title: Formal SignWriting in Practice
subtitle: Libraries, datasets, transformations, and implementation pathways
summary: Canonical statement of libraries, datasets, transformations, and real implementation pathways.
synopsis: The specification matters most when it supports real libraries, transformations, datasets, corpora, and implementation pathways. This entry states the stewardship layer in its smallest stable form and connects the model to the broader software and data ecosystem.
cover_image_alt: Cover image for Formal SignWriting in Practice in the Formal SignWriting series.

publication_plan.doi_strategy: publish as distinct citable artifacts connected by series identity and related identifiers

slides.title: Formal SignWriting in Practice
slides.summary: The technical model becomes durable when it is connected to real libraries, datasets, and implementation paths.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for Formal SignWriting in practice.

image_prompts.concrete: Research-software ecosystem graphic showing libraries, corpora, rendering, and shared tools connected through Formal SignWriting, clean and modern
image_prompts.abstract: One technical model branching into software, data, and maintenance pathways

slides.speech: A technical model should not end at abstract specification. Formal SignWriting has to be legible as a working implementation ecosystem, with libraries, datasets, transformations, and maintainable software paths. This is where the model becomes something future builders can inherit rather than rediscover.
slides.notes:
Connect the model to the actual ecosystem around it.
- libraries
- corpora and datasets
- transformations
- maintenance paths

slides.title: Real Libraries Make the Model Usable
slides.summary: A serious technical model should be traceable through real software artifacts.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for libraries and packages in practice.

image_prompts.concrete: Research software slide showing core processing libraries, rendering packages, and web components connected to Formal SignWriting, clean ecosystem diagram
image_prompts.abstract: A technical model turning into reusable tools and packages

bullets: Core libraries across JavaScript, Python, and PHP
slides.speech: Formal SignWriting matters more when it can be located in real software across more than one language and environment. Core libraries in JavaScript, Python, and PHP, together with font and rendering packages and reusable Web Components, turn the model into something that builders can reuse, maintain, and extend rather than only read about. The ecosystem becomes part of the technical record.
slides.notes:
Keep the connection to actual tooling concrete.
- JavaScript, Python, and PHP core libraries
- font and rendering packages
- reusable Web Components

flowchart TD

slides.title: Corpora Give the Model Consequences
slides.summary: The model becomes most valuable when it supports real collections, annotations, and reproducible workflows.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for corpora and workflows in practice.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical workflow slide showing lexicons, corpora, annotation pipelines, and search results built on Formal SignWriting, scholarly software design
image_prompts.abstract: Many signed data workflows converging into a reproducible technical practice

slides.speech: This is where the formal model proves itself outside isolated examples. It can support corpora, lexicons, annotation workflows, search pipelines, and reproducible technical practice. Those are not side effects. They are part of the reason the model matters, because they show what text that can be stored, searched, and reused across tools makes possible.
slides.notes: Tie practice to real research and maintenance value.

slides.title: A Maintainable Technical Home
slides.summary: Future builders need a stable model, a legible ecosystem, and a path forward.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for maintenance and continuity.
slides.speech: Formal SignWriting in practice is ultimately about stewardship. A durable model needs maintainable distinctions, clean transformations, and implementation habits that future builders can actually use. That is how technical continuity survives across packages, tools, and software generations, and how the perimeter can keep growing without destabilizing the center.
slides.notes:
Finish on continuity in practice.
- canonical versus derived data
- clean transformations
- maintainable implementations
- future builders inherit a legible system
